ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-25-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky appeared by zoom for William Taraday (Administrator).
Gary Rothstein appeared by zoom for Ron Hart (Executor of the Estate of Gail Hart).

Mr. Rothstein stated that he is not objecting to the granting of the petition. The Court stated that it is not happy about the fees having been paid without a Court order. Mr. Zabronsky stated that he believes the issue raised was addressed back in 2006 by Judge Dearman and that a consent was filed in April of 2006 which is attached to the petition. The Court stated that it should be attached to a request for payment. Mr. Zabronsky read language in the consents regarding taxes and the consent to payment of the administrative expenses and Attorney's fees, stated that the parties were instructed to pay these expenses, provided history and asked the Court to allow final distribution as requested. The Court noted that fees aren't due until 2021, spoke of the need for the Court to take some control and stated that right now the statutory fee is $188,000.00. Mr. Zabronsky stated that if extraordinary fees were claimed it would be hundreds and hundreds of thousands of dollars, spoke of time spent by the Administrator, stated that the Administrator advanced funds to the estate from his own pocket that he may not get back, and that Mr. Goldstein was unable to cover the expenses, so his Client stepped up. The Court stated that there's not even an acknowledgment of the statutory fees and that it does not see a request for extraordinary fees. Mr. Zabronsky referred to the supplemental declaration of William (paragraph 6 page 4), stated that the $188,000.00 figure came from them, that this has gone on for a very long time, that they are up against the gun and that if the income is distributed before March 5th, they will avoid taxes owed by the estate. The Court noted that this case has been around since 2001/for over 20-years and questions where the $200,000.00 number comes from. Mr. Zabronsky stated that it is so that his Client can be reimbursed for some and be a little bit closer in getting something out of this estate. The Amended Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Accountings, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Aug-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Levin for William Taraday (Administrator). James Sitkin for John J. Dacey (Objector).

The Court confirmed that the Parties have agreed to several amounts being paid and to several issues being put over for a later decision. Counsel had nothing to add with respect to Reed Smith's petition for fees and costs. Mr. Sitkin stated that Mr. Dacey will not oppose the award of $6,985.63 to OMW for tax fees. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Evan, Latham & Campisi's ("ELC") request for extraordinary fees and statutory fees, and stated that notice should have been given to Mr. Colb and to Jeffrey Taraday. The accounting petition was granted with respect to $24,753.75 to Clifton Larsen, $687.00 and $6,985.63 to OMW, $5,405.00 for costs to ELC, $6,772.50 to ELC for extraordinary fees, $825.00 to the Gwire firm and the amount of $50,805.00 for extraordinary fees to ELC are approved. The Court will not approve further statutory fees at this time without prejudice to a future request and will not allow fees and costs to Reed & Smith for the Petition for Further Review. The issues that are not decided in this order are withdrawn without prejudice to being subject to a future petition. Ms. Levin is to prepare a form of order.
